title: Stop a transaction retry from sending duplicate renewal email
prompt: Investigate `incident/job.log` together with `incident/audit.log`, then repair the duplicate renewal-receipt incident without changing the public API, incident evidence, or protected tests. The logs correlate two accepted provider deliveries for one logical receipt with a serialization failure and replay of the enclosing database transaction. External mail delivery must not happen inside a transaction attempt that can be retried. Stage the receipt at the existing durable transaction boundary instead: an aborted attempt must expose neither a mail delivery nor a committed queue entry, a successful retry must atomically commit the renewal and exactly one deterministic mail intent, and only the queue drainer may invoke the provider after commit. Preserve the transaction's retry limit and error propagation. Replaying an already completed job must remain idempotent and must not enqueue or deliver another receipt. Use only the Ruby standard library; do not add network access, randomness, wall-clock dependencies, sleeps, or subprocesses. Do not modify `protected_tests/` or `incident/`. Success is `sh protected_tests/verify.sh` passing from the repository root.
